speaker: LEONTES
text: Nor night nor day no rest. It is but weakness

speaker: LEONTES
text: To bear the matter thus, mere weakness. If

speaker: LEONTES
text: The cause were not in being—part o’ th’ cause,

speaker: LEONTES
text: She th’ adult’ress, for the harlot king

speaker: LEONTES
text: Is quite beyond mine arm, out of the blank

speaker: LEONTES
text: And level of my brain, plot-proof. But she

speaker: LEONTES
text: I can hook to me. Say that she were gone,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Given to the fire, a moiety of my rest

speaker: LEONTES
text: Might come to me again.—Who’s there?

speaker: SERVANT
text: My lord.

speaker: LEONTES
text: How does the boy?

speaker: SERVANT
text: He took good rest tonight. ’Tis hoped

speaker: SERVANT
text: His sickness is discharged.

speaker: LEONTES
text: To see his nobleness,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Conceiving the dishonor of his mother.

speaker: LEONTES
text: He straight declined, drooped, took it deeply,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Fastened and fixed the shame on ’t in himself,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Threw off his spirit, his appetite, his sleep,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And downright languished. Leave me solely. Go,

speaker: LEONTES
text: See how he fares.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Fie, fie, no thought of him.

speaker: LEONTES
text: The very thought of my revenges that way

speaker: LEONTES
text: Recoil upon me—in himself too mighty,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And in his parties, his alliance. Let him be

speaker: LEONTES
text: Until a time may serve. For present vengeance,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Take it on her. Camillo and Polixenes

speaker: LEONTES
text: Laugh at me, make their pastime at my sorrow.

speaker: LEONTES
text: They should not laugh if I could reach them, nor

speaker: LEONTES
text: Shall she within my power.

speaker: LORD
text: You must not enter.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Nay, rather, good my lords, be second to me.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Fear you his tyrannous passion more, alas,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Than the Queen’s life? A gracious innocent soul,

speaker: PAULINA
text: More free than he is jealous.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: That’s enough.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Madam, he hath not slept tonight, commanded

speaker: SERVANT
text: None should come at him.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Not so hot, good sir.

speaker: PAULINA
text: I come to bring him sleep. ’Tis such as you

speaker: PAULINA
text: That creep like shadows by him and do sigh

speaker: PAULINA
text: At each his needless heavings, such as you

speaker: PAULINA
text: Nourish the cause of his awaking. I

speaker: PAULINA
text: Do come with words as medicinal as true,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Honest as either, to purge him of that humor

speaker: PAULINA
text: That presses him from sleep.

speaker: LEONTES
text: What noise there, ho?

speaker: PAULINA
text: No noise, my lord, but needful conference

speaker: PAULINA
text: About some gossips for your Highness.

speaker: LEONTES
text: How?—

speaker: LEONTES
text: Away with that audacious lady. Antigonus,

speaker: LEONTES
text: I charged thee that she should not come about me.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I knew she would.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: I told her so, my lord,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: On your displeasure’s peril and on mine,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: She should not visit you.

speaker: LEONTES
text: What, canst not rule her?

speaker: PAULINA
text: From all dishonesty he can. In this,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Unless he take the course that you have done—

speaker: PAULINA
text: Commit me for committing honor—trust it,

speaker: PAULINA
text: He shall not rule me.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: La you now, you hear.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: When she will take the rein I let her run,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: But she’ll not stumble.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Good my liege, I come—

speaker: PAULINA
text: And I beseech you hear me, who professes

speaker: PAULINA
text: Myself your loyal servant, your physician,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Your most obedient counselor, yet that dares

speaker: PAULINA
text: Less appear so in comforting your evils

speaker: PAULINA
text: Than such as most seem yours—I say I come

speaker: PAULINA
text: From your good queen.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Good queen?

speaker: PAULINA
text: Good queen, my lord, good queen, I say “good queen,”

speaker: PAULINA
text: And would by combat make her good, so were I

speaker: PAULINA
text: A man, the worst about you.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Force her hence.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Let him that makes but trifles of his eyes

speaker: PAULINA
text: First hand me. On mine own accord I’ll off,

speaker: PAULINA
text: But first I’ll do my errand.—The good queen,

speaker: PAULINA
text: For she is good, hath brought you forth a daughter—

speaker: PAULINA
text: Here ’tis—commends it to your blessing.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Out!

speaker: LEONTES
text: A mankind witch! Hence with her, out o’ door.

speaker: LEONTES
text: A most intelligencing bawd.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Not so.

speaker: PAULINA
text: I am as ignorant in that as you

speaker: PAULINA
text: In so entitling me, and no less honest

speaker: PAULINA
text: Than you are mad—which is enough, I’ll warrant,

speaker: PAULINA
text: As this world goes, to pass for honest.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Traitors,

speaker: LEONTES
text:
Will you not push her out?
                 
                To
                 
                Antigonus
                .
               Give her the bastard,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Thou dotard; thou art woman-tired, unroosted

speaker: LEONTES
text: By thy Dame Partlet here. Take up the bastard,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Take ’t up, I say. Give ’t to thy crone.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Forever

speaker: PAULINA
text: Unvenerable be thy hands if thou

speaker: PAULINA
text: Tak’st up the Princess by that forced baseness

speaker: PAULINA
text: Which he has put upon ’t.

speaker: LEONTES
text: He dreads his wife.

speaker: PAULINA
text: So I would you did. Then ’twere past all doubt

speaker: PAULINA
text: You’d call your children yours.

speaker: LEONTES
text: A nest of traitors!

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: I am none, by this good light.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Nor I, nor any

speaker: PAULINA
text: But one that’s here, and that’s himself. For he

speaker: PAULINA
text: The sacred honor of himself, his queen’s,

speaker: PAULINA
text: His hopeful son’s, his babe’s, betrays to slander,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Whose sting is sharper than the sword’s; and will not—

speaker: PAULINA
text: For, as the case now stands, it is a curse

speaker: PAULINA
text: He cannot be compelled to ’t—once remove

speaker: PAULINA
text: The root of his opinion, which is rotten

speaker: PAULINA
text: As ever oak or stone was sound.

speaker: LEONTES
text: A callet

speaker: LEONTES
text: Of boundless tongue, who late hath beat her husband

speaker: LEONTES
text: And now baits me! This brat is none of mine.

speaker: LEONTES
text: It is the issue of Polixenes.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Hence with it, and together with the dam

speaker: LEONTES
text: Commit them to the fire.

speaker: PAULINA
text: It is yours,

speaker: PAULINA
text: And, might we lay th’ old proverb to your charge,

speaker: PAULINA
text: So like you ’tis the worse.—Behold, my lords,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Although the print be little, the whole matter

speaker: PAULINA
text: And copy of the father—eye, nose, lip,

speaker: PAULINA
text: The trick of ’s frown, his forehead, nay, the valley,

speaker: PAULINA
text: The pretty dimples of his chin and cheek, his smiles,

speaker: PAULINA
text: The very mold and frame of hand, nail, finger.

speaker: PAULINA
text: And thou, good goddess Nature, which hast made it

speaker: PAULINA
text: So like to him that got it, if thou hast

speaker: PAULINA
text: The ordering of the mind too, ’mongst all colors

speaker: PAULINA
text: No yellow in ’t, lest she suspect, as he does,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Her children not her husband’s.

speaker: LEONTES
text: A gross hag!—

speaker: LEONTES
text: And, losel, thou art worthy to be hanged

speaker: LEONTES
text: That wilt not stay her tongue.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Hang all the husbands

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: That cannot do that feat, you’ll leave yourself

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Hardly one subject.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Once more, take her hence.

speaker: PAULINA
text: A most unworthy and unnatural lord

speaker: PAULINA
text: Can do no more.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I’ll ha’ thee burnt.

speaker: PAULINA
text: I care not.

speaker: PAULINA
text: It is an heretic that makes the fire,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Not she which burns in ’t. I’ll not call you tyrant;

speaker: PAULINA
text: But this most cruel usage of your queen,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Not able to produce more accusation

speaker: PAULINA
text: Than your own weak-hinged fancy, something savors

speaker: PAULINA
text: Of tyranny, and will ignoble make you,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Yea, scandalous to the world.

speaker: LEONTES
text: On your allegiance,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Out of the chamber with her! Were I a tyrant,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Where were her life? She durst not call me so

speaker: LEONTES
text: If she did know me one. Away with her!

speaker: PAULINA
text: I pray you do not push me; I’ll be gone.—

speaker: PAULINA
text: Look to your babe, my lord; ’tis yours. Jove send her

speaker: PAULINA
text: A better guiding spirit.—What needs these hands?

speaker: PAULINA
text: You that are thus so tender o’er his follies

speaker: PAULINA
text: Will never do him good, not one of you.

speaker: PAULINA
text: So, so. Farewell, we are gone.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Thou, traitor, hast set on thy wife to this.

speaker: LEONTES
text: My child? Away with ’t! Even thou, that hast

speaker: LEONTES
text: A heart so tender o’er it, take it hence,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And see it instantly consumed with fire.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Even thou, and none but thou. Take it up straight.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Within this hour bring me word ’tis done,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And by good testimony, or I’ll seize thy life,

speaker: LEONTES
text: With what thou else call’st thine. If thou refuse

speaker: LEONTES
text: And wilt encounter with my wrath, say so.

speaker: LEONTES
text: The bastard brains with these my proper hands

speaker: LEONTES
text: Shall I dash out. Go, take it to the fire,

speaker: LEONTES
text: For thou sett’st on thy wife.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: I did not, sir.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: These lords, my noble fellows, if they please,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Can clear me in ’t.

speaker: LORDS
text: We can, my royal liege.

speaker: LORDS
text: He is not guilty of her coming hither.

speaker: LEONTES
text: You’re liars all.

speaker: LORD
text: Beseech your Highness, give us better credit.

speaker: LORD
text: We have always truly served you, and beseech

speaker: LORD
text: So to esteem of us. And on our knees we beg,

speaker: LORD
text: As recompense of our dear services

speaker: LORD
text: Past and to come, that you do change this purpose,

speaker: LORD
text: Which being so horrible, so bloody, must

speaker: LORD
text: Lead on to some foul issue. We all kneel.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I am a feather for each wind that blows.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Shall I live on to see this bastard kneel

speaker: LEONTES
text: And call me father? Better burn it now

speaker: LEONTES
text: Than curse it then. But be it; let it live.

speaker: LEONTES
text:
It shall not neither.
                 
                To
                 
                Antigonus
                .
               You, sir, come you hither,

speaker: LEONTES
text: You that have been so tenderly officious

speaker: LEONTES
text: With Lady Margery, your midwife there,

speaker: LEONTES
text: To save this bastard’s life—for ’tis a bastard,

speaker: LEONTES
text: So sure as this beard’s gray. What will you adventure

speaker: LEONTES
text: To save this brat’s life?

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Anything, my lord,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: That my ability may undergo

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: And nobleness impose. At least thus much:

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: I’ll pawn the little blood which I have left

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: To save the innocent. Anything possible.

speaker: LEONTES
text: It shall be possible. Swear by this sword

speaker: LEONTES
text: Thou wilt perform my bidding.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: I will, my lord.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Mark, and perform it, seest thou; for the fail

speaker: LEONTES
text: Of any point in ’t shall not only be

speaker: LEONTES
text: Death to thyself but to thy lewd-tongued wife,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Whom for this time we pardon. We enjoin thee,

speaker: LEONTES
text: As thou art liegeman to us, that thou carry

speaker: LEONTES
text: This female bastard hence, and that thou bear it

speaker: LEONTES
text: To some remote and desert place quite out

speaker: LEONTES
text: Of our dominions, and that there thou leave it,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Without more mercy, to it own protection

speaker: LEONTES
text: And favor of the climate. As by strange fortune

speaker: LEONTES
text: It came to us, I do in justice charge thee,

speaker: LEONTES
text: On thy soul’s peril and thy body’s torture,

speaker: LEONTES
text: That thou commend it strangely to some place

speaker: LEONTES
text: Where chance may nurse or end it. Take it up.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: I swear to do this, though a present death

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Had been more merciful.—Come on, poor babe.

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Some powerful spirit instruct the kites and ravens

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: To be thy nurses! Wolves and bears, they say,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Casting their savageness aside, have done

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text:
Like offices of pity.
                 
                To
                 
                Leontes
                .
               Sir, be prosperous

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: In more than this deed does require.—And blessing

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Against this cruelty fight on thy side,

speaker: ANTIGONUS
text: Poor thing, condemned to loss.

speaker: LEONTES
text: No, I’ll not rear

speaker: LEONTES
text: Another’s issue.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Please your Highness, posts

speaker: SERVANT
text: From those you sent to th’ oracle are come

speaker: SERVANT
text: An hour since. Cleomenes and Dion,

speaker: SERVANT
text: Being well arrived from Delphos, are both landed,

speaker: SERVANT
text: Hasting to th’ court.

speaker: LORD
text: So please you, sir, their speed

speaker: LORD
text: Hath been beyond account.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Twenty-three days

speaker: LEONTES
text: They have been absent. ’Tis good speed, foretells

speaker: LEONTES
text: The great Apollo suddenly will have

speaker: LEONTES
text: The truth of this appear. Prepare you, lords.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Summon a session, that we may arraign

speaker: LEONTES
text: Our most disloyal lady; for, as she hath

speaker: LEONTES
text: Been publicly accused, so shall she have

speaker: LEONTES
text: A just and open trial. While she lives,

speaker: LEONTES
text: My heart will be a burden to me. Leave me,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And think upon my bidding.
